---
title: "Euromech 670"
description: "J-M. Allain and T. Lavigne presented their work in the field of cornea mechanics and porous media mechanics at Euromech 670 in Rennes."
author: "T. Lavigne"
date: "2026-04-10"          
categories: ["Conference"] 
image: "../resources/images/news/euromech_670.png"          
draft: false  
format: html
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: solana
  image-width: 20em
  image-shape: rounded
---

<style>
  /* Add horizontal space between image and text on desktop screens */
  @media (min-width: 768px) {
    .quarto-about-solana .about-image {
      margin-left: 3rem !important; /* Increase this number for even more space */
    }
  }
</style>

:::{#hero-heading}
:::


## Event Summary

We are excited to share that **Jean-Marc Allain** and **Thomas Lavigne** recently represented our research group at the **Euromech 670** Colloquium held in Rennes. 

During the conference, they presented our latest computational and experimental findings across two of our primary research themes:

* **Cornea Mechanics:** J.-M. Allain presented recent insights into the intricate multiscale mechanical behavior of the human cornea, detailing how lamellar microstructures influence the tissue's response to surgical interventions and pathology.
* **Porous Media Mechanics:** T. Lavigne highlighted advanced frameworks for poromechanical modeling, demonstrating the impact of mechanical loading and fluid dynamics on soft tissue microstructures.

The colloquium provided an excellent platform to share our cutting-edge methodologies with the broader mechanics community and to foster new collaborations in the field of soft matter and tissue engineering.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Conference Program & Book of Abstracts:** [Download PDF](https://670.euromech.org/documents/266/Euromech_670_documents_mZEIiIG.pdf)
:::
